<insights>

- Isolated files — no import edges between 16 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0)
- 16 markdown files with zero cohesion and coupling is expected — documentation has no import graph participation.
- The docs cover memory architecture, PRD steward vision, and level-system reference — suggesting the project has a mature internal design language worth preserving in onboarding materials.
- Documents like 2026-03-03-refresh-orchestration-memory-analysis.md use date-prefixed naming, indicating a log-style ADR (Architecture Decision Record) practice.
- Date-prefixed filenames (e.g. 2026-03-03-refresh-orchestration-memory-analysis.md) establish an informal ADR practice — consider adopting a lightweight ADR template to make decision context and consequences consistent across documents.
- The docs zone covers memory-architecture, memory-risks, and memory-improvements as separate files — these could be consolidated into a single memory-architecture.md to reduce fragmentation and improve discoverability.
- Zero coupling from docs to source is correct — no documentation file should import source code, and this zone cleanly enforces that boundary.
- Documentation is fully decoupled from all source zones with zero import edges — but several docs describe architecture (memory-architecture.md, prd-steward-vision.md) that could drift from actual implementation without any tooling to detect divergence.
- Architecture docs (memory-architecture.md, level-system-reference.md) describe runtime behaviors and design patterns that have no mechanical link to the source zones they describe — consider adding a CI step (e.g. sourcevision validate) that flags when source structure diverges significantly from documented zone boundaries.
- None of the architecture docs reference the zone IDs produced by sourcevision — docs describe architectural concepts (tiers, gateways) but use human names rather than zone IDs, making it impossible to cross-reference a doc against its corresponding zone metrics without manual mapping.
